item.title: Alex Imas and Phil Trammell – What remains scarce after AGI? 摘要
item.description:
Dwarkesh Patel interviews economists Alex Imas (Google DeepMind, UChicago) and Phil Trammell (Epoch, Stanford) on AGI economics. They discuss what will remain scarce after AGI, including human-provided relational services. Imas argues individual forecasts are unreliable and advocates for prediction markets and data collection. They explore scenarios for labor vs. capital share, tax and redistribution of AI wealth, and why demand collapse is unlikely. The conversation covers the 'messy middle' scenario, human integration into a machine economy, and strategies for developing countries.
标签: AGI, economics, AI, automation, labor, capital, scarcity, redistribution, prediction markets, future of work

item.title: Quoting Emanuel Maiberg, 404 Media 摘要
item.description:
Google employees internally share memes criticizing the quality of the company's AI products, reflecting internal dissatisfaction. After publication, Google's spokesperson requested a revised statement that removed the phrase 'it's critical that we maintain humans in the loop,' suggesting a shift in the company's public stance on human oversight in AI.
标签: AI, Google, AI ethics, journalism, internal culture, quality, human oversight, tech criticism

item.title: Integrating smooth periodic functions 摘要
item.description:
The function f(x) = cos(sin(x) + x) is smooth, periodic with period 2π, and has zero first five derivatives at odd multiples of π. Due to this flatness, the trapezoid rule for numerical integration converges exponentially rather than quadratically. With only 16 integration points, the error reaches machine precision, far exceeding the standard error bound of 82.67/N². This demonstrates the efficiency of the trapezoid rule for smooth periodic functions.
标签: numerical integration, trapezoid rule, periodic functions, smooth functions, error analysis, exponential convergence, floating point, mathematics

item.title: Google’s Gemini Mac App Is Native, in a Distinctly Google Way, But Annoyingly Presumptuous 摘要
item.description:
Google's native Gemini Mac app installs background helpers (GeminiAppLauncher, GoogleUpdater) without permission and silently re-adds them if removed, with no opt-out setting. The author finds this disrespectful and prefers ChatGPT's Mac client. The app was built using a Java-to-Objective-C converter, originally for Android. The author ultimately deleted the app and its launch agents.
标签: Google, Gemini, Mac, privacy, software, AI, app review, background processes, user experience

item.title: Remember When Chrome Went Bad on MacOS? 摘要
item.description:
Loren Brichter reported in 2020 that Google Chrome's Keystone updater caused massive CPU usage in macOS WindowServer, slowing down Macs even when Chrome wasn't running. Deleting Chrome and Keystone restored performance. The issue was eventually fixed, but the article argues that third-party apps should not install background agents for unnecessary updates, contrasting macOS's openness with iOS's locked-down approach that prevents such problems.
标签: Chrome, macOS, performance, Keystone, Google, background agents, software updates, Apple, iOS, WindowServer

item.title: Lingon and Lingon Pro 10 摘要
item.description:
Lingon and Lingon Pro are Mac apps for scheduling apps, scripts, shortcuts, and commands with a user-friendly interface. They allow running tasks at specific times, intervals, or login, with optional notifications. The free version is simpler, while Pro is a paid upgrade with more features. The article highlights how Lingon can simplify monitoring and relaunching apps like Maestral, and also notes its utility for inspecting login items and background agents, such as those installed by Google Gemini.
标签: Mac, utility, scheduling, automation, Lingon, productivity, macOS, scripts, background agents

item.title: The Emacs packages that I use (as of June 2026) 摘要
item.description:
The author provides an updated overview of their Emacs 30.2 configuration, highlighting key packages and changes since late 2023. They use Flyspell for text-mode spell checking, Eglot as the LSP server for Go, Python, shell, and C programming, and Flycheck with Flymake for diagnostics. Completion is handled by vertico, marginalia, orderless, consult, corfu, and completion-preview-mode. Tramp is configured for remote file access. Additional packages include flyover, sideline, and flycheck-inline for diagnostics display. Unchanged packages include embark, Magit, git-timemachine, diminish, backward-forward, which-key, vundo, smartparens, and try. Evil was removed due to conflicts with existing Emacs reflexes.
标签: Emacs, configuration, packages, LSP, Eglot, Flycheck, completion, Magit, Tramp, programming

item.title: The Latin of Linux 摘要
item.description:
The article draws an analogy between Latin's influence on modern languages and ed(1)'s influence on Unix command-line tools. Just as Latin explains many linguistic conventions, ed(1) explains conventions in sed, awk, grep, vi, perl, bash, and others. The post lists ten features originating from ed(1), including slash-delimited regex, $ for end-of-line, address+action patterns, regex as address ranges, backreferences, & for matched text, g/re/p pattern, p for printing, vi commands (a, c, d, i, j, l, p, q, r, w), and ! for shell escape. The author notes that ed(1) is a convenient starting point for Unix history, similar to Latin for Western linguistics.
标签: ed, Unix, command-line, history, regular expressions, Linux, sed, awk, grep, vi

item.title: ‘Microsoft and OpenAI Broke Up — Now They’re Ready to Fight’ 摘要
item.description:
Microsoft's AI chief Mustafa Suleyman stated the company aims to become one of the top four AI labs globally, acknowledging it currently trails behind Google DeepMind, OpenAI, and Anthropic. The Verge article compares Microsoft's Build conference to a 'freshly single divorcée posting a thirst trap,' highlighting the company's renewed independence after its breakup with OpenAI. However, the author expresses skepticism, drawing parallels to Microsoft's long-standing but unsuccessful efforts to compete with Google Search via Bing.
标签: Microsoft, AI, Build conference, Mustafa Suleyman, OpenAI, Google DeepMind, Anthropic, competition, tech industry, AI labs

item.title: ‘The Insider’ 摘要
item.description:
The author reflects on the current controversy surrounding 60 Minutes, drawing parallels to the 1999 film 'The Insider,' which depicts a whistleblower's ordeal with Big Tobacco. They contrast the film's scenario—where tobacco companies used legal threats—with today's situation, where they imagine the network being bought and its staff replaced by pro-tobacco figures. The post includes a link to the original 60 Minutes segment on Big Tobacco.
标签: 60 Minutes, The Insider, Michael Mann, Big Tobacco, media, journalism, whistleblower, film, current events

item.title: The Talk Show Live From WWDC 2026: Tuesday in San Jose 摘要
item.description:
The Talk Show will host a live audience episode during WWDC 2026 at the California Theatre in San Jose on Tuesday, June 9, 2026, at 7pm PT. Tickets are $45, and doors open at 6pm. Special guests are confirmed. The event encourages attendees to mingle with fellow Daring Fireball readers and listeners before the show.
标签: WWDC, live show, podcast, San Jose, tech event, Daring Fireball, The Talk Show, tickets, Apple

item.title: Using Safetensors with Flax 摘要
item.description:
The author is porting a PyTorch LLM to JAX/Flax and wants to use Safetensors for checkpoints. The Safetensors Flax API (actually a JAX API) expects flat dictionaries mapping strings to arrays, but Flax's `nnx.State.to_pure_dict` produces nested dicts. Feeding nested dicts causes Safetensors to wrap them in NumPy object arrays, leading to a 'dtype object is not covered' error. The solution is to flatten the state using `nnx.to_flat_state`, which produces tuples of path keys and values, then convert to a flat dict with dot-separated keys for Safetensors.
标签: JAX, Flax, Safetensors, checkpointing, LLM, deep learning, serialization, debugging, Python, NumPy

item.title: IPv6 zones in URLs are a mistake 摘要
item.description:
IPv6 link-local addresses (fe80::/10) use zone IDs (e.g., %eth0) to disambiguate interfaces. In URLs, IPv6 addresses are enclosed in brackets, but the percent sign in zone IDs conflicts with URL percent-encoding. Go's net/url parser rejects '%eth0' as an invalid escape. The correct encoding is '%25' for the percent sign (e.g., http://[fe80::4%25eth0]:80). This behavior is specified in RFC 6874, but many tools (nginx, Python requests) struggle with it. Browsers avoid IPv6 zones due to origin model complications, and a draft RFC attempts to address this.
标签: IPv6, URL encoding, percent-encoding, Go, networking, RFC 6874, zone ID, link-local address, web standards, edge case
